edge: Federated Distillation (FD) has recently attracted attention for its efficiency in aggregating multiple diverse local models trained from statistically heterogeneous data of distributed clients. Existing FD methods generally treat these models equally by merely computing the average of their output soft predictions.

In this paper, a new perspective is proposed that treats the local data in each client as a specific domain and designs a novel domain knowledge aware federated distillation method, dubbed DaFKD, that can discern the importance of each model to the distillation sample.
Specifically, a domain discriminator is employed for each client, which is trained to identify the correlation factor between the sample and the corresponding domain.
To facilitate the training of the domain discriminator while saving communication costs, its partial parameters are proposed to be shared with the classification model.
The proposed method can improve the model accuracy by up to 6.02% compared to state-of-the-art baselines.

The model's capability to make accurate predictions depends on the sample being from the same domain as the model is trained on. The correlation between the model's domain and a distillation sample needs to be quantified.

Inspired by adversarial training, where a discriminator is used to differentiate between real data and generated data, a domain discriminator is proposed in this paper to evaluate the correlation between a distillation sample and the local domain.

The paper tackles the data heterogeneity challenge in federated knowledge distillation.
The paper proposes a novel method called domain-aware federated knowledge distillation, namely, DaFKD.
A domain discriminator is used to compute the correlation between the distillation sample and the domain for training the local model.
The partial parameters of the domain discriminator are shared with the classification model to facilitate training.
Extensive experiments conducted on various datasets and settings show that the method achieves significant improvement in model accuracy.
